feedback.text: Please, make "Stop Destroying Videogames" happen. game studios / publishers should not have the ability to make a game that was purchased unplayable. I am not saying companies should support their titles indefinitely. But there needs to be a way for the game to be playable after end of support. Game studios should have a end of life plan, ideally early in development so that their games have the ability to be played without their intervention. there are lots of ways to do those things. give server binaries to the Community (if possible), make server code open source (if possible). simply design the game with offline play in mind. Minecraft is a perfect exemple of how it can be done, Microsoft has oficial server open so that people can play together.However mojang (the developer before Microsoft acquisition) knew that they might not have the ability to support the game after launch so they did the following: 1 - the game does not need internet connection for offline play. 2 - the game supports LAN (local area network) multiplayer. so that player can play together without the need for dedicated server if they are on the same LAN network. 3 - server binaries are given to the community, so players themselves can host they own servers for the game. Because of those features of minecraft even if Microsoft decided to stop supporting the game tomorrow the game would still be 100% playable even without any Microsoft involvement. Sincerely,
